Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 017 (+) [Erra and Išum 2][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] a-[šar ...]

[...] place.

o 22

[... dA.NUN].NA.KI iš-[ta-kan ...]

He set [...] Anunnaku.

o 33

[...] ⸢e?⸣-ru-um-ma ⸢it-ta⸣-[ziz ...]

He entered [...] and stood [...].

o 44

[...]-ri-šu-ma ša₂-ru-ri-šu u₂-šam-⸢qit-ma?⸣ x IA ⸢RU KU⸣ x [...]

[...] ... him and caused his brilliance to fade and ... [...].

o 55

[...] a-šar-ša₂-nam-ma pa-⸢nu⸣-šu šak-⸢nu⸣-ma KItu₂ ul x [...]

[...] set his face to another place and did not ... [...] the earth.

o 66

[...] it-bu-nim-ma <u₄-mu> nam-⸢ru ana⸣ da-⸢um?-ma?⸣-ti ut-⸢tir⸣

[...] got up and the bright day was turned into darkness.

o 77

[...] ⸢UN⸣-MEŠ ina KUR nap-⸢ḫar⸣-ša₂ [...] ⸢TI? KIN⸣ [...]

[...] of the people in all of the land [...] ... [...].

o 88

[... i]-⸢ru⸣-ru-ma e-te-[lu-u₂ ...]

[...] trembled and went up [...].

o 99

[... i]-⸢ši?-ḫu-ma⸣ [...]

[...] grew and [...].

(19 lines missing)
o 1'1'

⸢i-na la⸣ [...]

"At a not [...]

o 2'2'

⸢a⸣-na sa-⸢pan⸣ [...]

"To flatten [...]"

o 3'3'

de₂-a ⸢LUGAL⸣ [...]

King Ea [...]:

o 4'4'

en-na ša₂ it-⸢bu⸣-[u ...]

"Now that he got up [...],

o 5'5'

ša₂ um-ma-ni šu-nu-ti ⸢e?⸣-[la-šu₂-nu ...]

"[...] the going up of those craftsmen.

o 6'6'

ṣal-⸢mi⸣-šu₂-nu ša₂ <ina> UN-MEŠ ab-nu-⸢u₂⸣ [...]

"[...] their images that I created among the people [...]

o 7'7'

⸢ša₂⸣ [DINGIR] la iʾ-ir-ru i-ṭe₄-⸢eḫ⸣-[ḫu-u ...]

"(they) approach [...] where [a god] cannot go?"

o 8'8'

[a-na um-ma]-⸢ni⸣ šu-nu-ti ŠA₃ rap-ša₂ id-din!(TI-)šu-nu!(AŠ-)⸢ti?⸣ [...]

He gave those craftsmen a wide heart [...]

o 9'9'

[uz-ni] ⸢iš⸣-ruk-šu-nu-ti-ma qa-ti-šu₂-⸢nu⸣ u₂-[lal-li]

He presented them with [intelligence] and enriched their hands.

o 10'10'

[šu-kut]-⸢ta⸣ šu-a-ša₂ u₂-ša₂-an-bi-ṭu-ma ⸢šum⸣-su-qat₃!(KAD₃) <UGU> ša₂ ⸢maḫ⸣-[ri]

They made that šukuttu-adornment shine so that it was even more select than before.

o 11'11'

[qu-ra]-⸢du⸣ dER₃.RA mu-ša₂ u ur-ra la na-par-⸢ka⸣-a u₂-zu-uz pa-⸢ni⸣-[šu₂]

Warrior Erra is standing before it, day and night without ceasing.

o 12'12'

[... ana] ⸢šu-un⸣-bu-uṭ šu-kut-tu₂ a-na ma-⸢li⸣-kut ma-al-[ki]  ; [...]-x-ki-⸢nu⸣-ma i-ta-mu-u₂ la ta-⸢ṭe⸣-[ḫi] ⸢a-na šip-ri⸣

[... to] make the šukuttu-adornment shine for the rulership of the prince [...] and say: 'Do not come near the work.'

o 13'13'

[...]-⸢šu⸣ na-piš-⸢ta⸣-šu₂ a-na-kis-⸢ma ni-kis⸣-[su ...]

[...] ... I will sever his throat and his cut [...].

o 14'14'

[...] x NAK x U₂ ⸢MA⸣ x [...] x x [...]

[...] ... [...] ... [...].

o 15'15'

[...] x x x [...] ul [...]

[...] ... [...] not [...].

o 16'16'

[...] u ⸢d⸣ER₃.[RA ...]

[...] and Erra [...].

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
(start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[...] x x [...]

[...] ... [...].

r 2'2'

⸢ul⸣-tu IRI ⸢ana IRI ZU?⸣ [...]

From town to town ... [...].

r 3'3'

DUMU ša₂ AD za-ru-u ša₂ DUMU [...]

A son from a father, a begetter from a son [...]

r 4'4'

AMA ša₂ DUMU.MUNUS ina ṣi-ḫa-[a-ti ...]

A mother [...] of a daughter with laughter.

r 5'5'

⸢ana⸣ šu-bat a-šar la a-ri x [...]

To a dwelling, an inaccessible place, ... [...].

r 6'6'

[ana] šu-bat NUN-MEŠ u₂-⸢šeš⸣-[šeb? ...]

I will settle [... in] a dwelling of princes.

r 7'7'

⸢u₂⸣-ma-mi na-si-⸢ka⸣-[ti ...]

[...] the beasts from the faraway lands.

r 8'8'

[IRI] ⸢in-nam⸣-ma-⸢ru⸣ [...]

[The city] (where) they appear [...].

(missing)
(end reverse blank)
